drills.name: 3-Man Weave
drills.description: Three players weave down the field passing to each other while moving laterally, finishing with a shot on goal. Focus on crisp passes and leading teammates into space.
drills.setup: Three lines at baseline, move down the field in a weaving pattern
progression: Add second weave group for competition
drills.benefits: Develops passing accuracy, timing, and field awareness under pressure

drills.name: Dodging Gates
drills.description: Players navigate through cones set in a grid pattern, practicing various dodge types (face dodge, bull dodge, split dodge) at each gate.
coaching_points: Keep ball on strong hand side
drills.setup: Create grid of cones 5 yards apart, players move through weaving pattern
progression: Game speed with random dodge selection
Add passive defenders at each gate
drills.benefits: Improves footwork, stick control, and ability to create space

drills.name: Quick Stick Shooting
drills.description: Partners feed ball to shooter who immediately releases shot within 1-2 seconds. Emphasizes quick release and accuracy from 10-15 yards.
drills.setup: One feeder, one shooter, goalie in goal. Rotate through line
drills.benefits: Develops quick release, shot accuracy, and decision-making under time pressure

drills.name: Ground Ball Outs
drills.description: Coach rolls loose balls out to waiting players who must scoop and sprint to baseline (end line), then transition back for outlet pass.
coaching_points: Sprint at full speed to outlet
drills.setup: Four corners of field, coach at midfield rolling balls out
progression: Double outs with two balls in play
drills.benefits: Improves ground ball technique, transition speed, and conditioning

drills.name: Defensive Positioning Drill
drills.description: Defender stays between attacker and goal, adjusting position based on ball location. Focus on body positioning, stick placement, and leverage.
coaching_points: Keep attacker on weak hand side when possible
drills.setup: Attacker-defender pairs in confined area near goal
drills.benefits: Builds defensive fundamentals, body awareness, and anticipation

drills.name: Faceoff Preparation
drills.description: Two FOGOs practice clamping technique, hand positioning, footwork, and the follow-through needed to win ground balls immediately after the clamp.
coaching_points: Explosive leg drive to ground ball
drills.setup: Two FOGOs at X facing each other with stick
progression: Compete for ground ball after clamp
drills.benefits: Develops faceoff technique, strength, and ground ball transition

drills.name: Transition 3v2 Outlet
drills.description: Defensive unit (3 players) breaks out against incoming attackers (2). Emphasizes quick outlet pass and communication.
drills.setup: 3 defenders with 2 attackers incoming, outlet receivers waiting
drills.benefits: Develops transition speed, outlet passing accuracy, and defensive communication

drills.name: Off-Hand Shooting
drills.description: Players practice shooting exclusively with their off-hand (non-dominant side) from various distances and angles.
coaching_points: Same fundamental mechanics as strong hand
drills.setup: Shooters line up on off-hand side (left side for right-handed players)
drills.benefits: Develops ambidextrous shooting ability and offensive flexibility

drills.name: Crease Attack Finish
drills.description: Attackers practice finishing inside crease area with various feeding scenarios: dodge-and-finish, feed from X, feed from sideline.
drills.setup: Feeders at X and wing, attackers inside crease, goalie in goal
drills.benefits: Develops crease awareness, quick-stick technique, and high-percentage finishing

drills.name: Man-Down Defense
drills.description: Five defensive players defend against six offensive players (numerical disadvantage). Focus on communication, positioning, and shot pressure.
drills.setup: 5v6 inside offensive zone with 2 attacks and 4 defenders plus goalie
drills.benefits: Builds defensive organization, communication, and handling numerical disadvantage

drills.name: Inside Feed Drill
drills.description: Wing players feed attackers inside crease. Emphasizes accurate feeding angles, leading passes, and timing.
coaching_points: Deliver ball slightly ahead of receiver
Use high-low feed when heavily marked
drills.setup: Wings at sideline, attackers in crease area, goalie in goal
drills.benefits: Develops passing accuracy, field awareness, and timing between teammates

drills.name: Goalie Clear Drill
drills.description: Goalie practices clearing balls to designated outlets after save. Develop quick decision-making and accurate long passes.
drills.setup: Goalie in goal with three outlets positioned at different spots
drills.benefits: Improves goalie clearance accuracy, decision-making, and initiation of fast break

drills.name: Pick-and-Roll Offense
drills.description: Offensive players use screen-and-roll concepts to create scoring opportunities. Develops spacing and ball movement.
drills.setup: Full offensive unit running set plays with screens
drills.benefits: Develops team offensive concepts, spacing, and complex ball movement

drills.name: Save Reaction Drill
drills.description: Coaches shoot rapid-fire shots at goalie from various distances and angles to develop reflexes and reaction time.
drills.setup: Coach or feeder at wing positions shooting at goalie
drills.benefits: Develops goalie reflexes, reaction time, and handling high-volume shot attempts

drills.name: Split Dodge Series
drills.description: Players practice split dodge (changing directions sharply) against passive then active defenders, building elusive footwork.
drills.setup: One-on-one in confined area with defender applying varying pressure
drills.benefits: Develops ability to change direction, create space, and handle pressure

drills.name: Wing-to-Attack Feed
drills.description: Midfield wings pass to three attackers in organized set, developing feed timing and angle recognition.
drills.setup: Wings and attackers positioned in offensive set with goalie
drills.benefits: Develops wing-to-attack feeding accuracy and timing in offensive set

drills.name: Scooping and Securing
drills.description: Players practice fundamental scooping technique with focus on proper hand positioning, footwork, and ball control.
drills.setup: Loose balls scattered in line, players move through scooping them
drills.benefits: Builds fundamental scooping technique, speed, and ball security

drills.name: Check and Stick Control
drills.description: Defenders practice stick checks with proper form, protecting hands while applying stick pressure to ball carrier.
coaching_points: Keep hands low and behind stick
drills.setup: One-on-one defender versus cradler in confined area
drills.benefits: Develops proper stick checking technique and defensive stick control

drills.name: Midfield Transition Drill
drills.description: Midfielders practice transitioning from defense to offense with focus on speed, communication, and maintaining spacing.
drills.setup: Full midfield group split into transitioning units
drills.benefits: Develops midfield transition speed, spacing awareness, and decision-making

drills.name: Goalie 1v1 Advantage
drills.description: Goalie leaves crease to play 1v1 against one-on-one situation, developing confidence and positioning outside crease.
drills.setup: One-on-one attacker versus goalie with no crease restrictions
drills.benefits: Builds goalie confidence, positioning, and ability to handle one-on-one situations

drills.name: Ground Ball Outs with Pressure
drills.description: Players compete for ground balls rolled out with active defenders pursuing, simulating game-speed ground ball situations.
drills.setup: Ground balls rolled out with multiple competitors
drills.benefits: Develops ground ball wins under pressure and transition speed

drills.name: Offensive Shape Drill
drills.description: Full offensive unit practices maintaining proper spacing and shape while moving the ball without live defenders.
drills.setup: Offensive unit in organized set moving ball according to pattern
drills.benefits: Develops offensive understanding of spacing, movement, and ball distribution

drills.name: Defensive Slide
drills.description: Defenders practice lateral sliding movement to stay in front of moving ball carrier without crossing feet.
drills.setup: Partner moving along line, defender maintains distance with slides
drills.benefits: Builds fundamental defensive footwork and lateral movement

drills.name: X-Attack Drill
drills.description: Attack player at X position receives various feeds and finishes, simulating feeding from wings and midfielders.
drills.setup: Attack at X with feeders at wing and top positions
drills.benefits: Develops X positioning, feed recognition, and finishing ability

drills.name: Conditioning and Transition
drills.description: High-intensity drill combining conditioning with game-speed transitions, simulating quarters with minimal rest.
drills.setup: Continuous transitions with short rest periods
drills.benefits: Builds cardiovascular conditioning and maintains game speed when fatigued

drills.name: Face-Off Clamp Technique
drills.description: FOGOs practice pure clamping technique and stick control at X without competition, focusing on proper mechanics.
drills.setup: Two FOGOs at X with coach directing motion
drills.benefits: Develops proper faceoff technique and muscle memory

drills.name: One-on-One Attack vs Defense
drills.description: Live one-on-one competition inside offensive zone, developing defensive and offensive 1v1 skills against full resistance.
drills.setup: Small field area for confined 1v1 play
drills.benefits: Develops 1v1 skills, competitiveness, and decision-making under pressure

drills.name: Zone Defensive Principles
drills.description: Defensive unit practices zone defense concepts - defending areas rather than players, with proper rotation and communication.
drills.setup: Full defensive unit against offensive set in zone formation
drills.benefits: Develops zone understanding, communication, and team defensive principles

drills.name: Cradle and Protect
drills.description: Players practice proper cradling technique and ball protection against pressure, developing fundamental ball control.
drills.setup: Pairs with one player cradling against passive pressure
drills.benefits: Builds fundamental ball control and protection under pressure

drills.name: Fast Break Sequence
drills.description: Players execute fast break opportunities from defensive possession to shot, emphasizing speed, spacing, and finishing.
drills.setup: Defensive possession triggers fast break to offensive end
drills.benefits: Develops fast break execution, transition speed, and finishing ability

organization_guide.skill_progression: Drills progress from fundamental techniques (Scooping and Securing, Cradle and Protect) to game situations (Fast Break, Zone Defense)
